title: Build agreement and journey review experiences

environment.os: Windows local; GitHub Actions Ubuntu and Windows pending

commands.name: Missing requirement and journey UI gate module
commands.expectedFailure: The focused example suite could not import the not-yet-created review-experience module

Complete corrected-head deterministic verification on the combined UX-01 and UX-02 tree

scenarios.scenario: The privacy officer edits and confirms a source-bound test rule
An unclear proposal remains visibly ambiguous and non-executable
A rejected proposal remains in immutable history
A read-only reviewer can inspect the source but cannot record a requirement decision
Agreement review has no automatically detectable accessibility violations
Every requirement decision remains reachable by keyboard
The officer creates a runnable teacher journey with a causal chain
A student journey edit appends immutable version history
Required visibility cannot be omitted from a runnable journey
Journey review has no automatically detectable accessibility violations
Required journey visibility remains reachable by keyboard

visualEvidence.rationale: UX-02 changes agreement and journey review, authority explanations, citation navigation, role denial, decision readiness, responsive layouts, keyboard focus, and replay/repair empty states. Twelve source-bound optimized-production screenshots cover those visible states.

assets.altText: Desktop agreement review with a complete fictional rule draft, exact quote, required-source message, and all three decision controls disabled before the cited page is opened.
assets.caption: Complete review fields cannot bypass the exact-source inspection gate.

assets.altText: Focused stored agreement page containing the exact fictional classroom-purpose sentence, page offsets, hash, and a return-to-reviewed-draft button.
assets.caption: Citation navigation lands on the deterministic stored page rather than trusting the model quote alone.

assets.altText: Desktop source-beside-rule review with citation inspected, decision ready, and a visible cyan keyboard-focus ring on Confirm as test rule.
assets.caption: Confirm becomes keyboard reachable only after source inspection and complete human input.

assets.altText: Desktop source-beside-rule review with a visible keyboard-focus ring on Mark ambiguous while source and human rationale remain visible.
assets.caption: Keyboard order reaches the non-executable ambiguity decision after Confirm.

assets.altText: Desktop source-beside-rule review with a visible keyboard-focus ring on Reject draft and the exact fictional citation still present.
assets.caption: Keyboard order reaches Reject without changing or hiding the source-bound review.

assets.altText: Desktop human-confirmed executable test rule beside its exact fictional source, reviewer rationale, direct model-human-browser authority explanation, and immutable history.
assets.caption: A named person creates a bounded test instruction; the screen does not call it a legal or safety conclusion.

assets.altText: Immutable requirement history with human-confirmed version 2 above preserved non-executable model proposal version 1, including actors, times, rationale, and change count.
assets.caption: Human review appends a version without rewriting the model draft or its citation.

assets.altText: Narrow agreement review with three stacked authority explanations and an ambiguous non-executable human decision beside the exact fictional source and immutable history.
assets.caption: Ambiguity remains direct, non-executable, source-bound, and readable at the narrow viewport.

assets.altText: Desktop rejected non-executable model draft beside its exact fictional source, recorded human rationale, actor, time, and preserved two-version history.
assets.caption: Rejection cannot create an executable rule or erase proposal provenance.

assets.altText: Desktop teacher journey with authority explanation, current rule, active authorization, fictional teacher, allowed and prohibited actions, required checkpoint, causal chain, immutable version, and separate empty replay and repair histories.
assets.caption: The complete journey configuration shows why it is runnable without implying that a browser run occurred.

assets.altText: Narrow student journey with fictional actor, direct authority explanation, actions, required visibility, causal chain, two immutable versions, and separate replay and repair empty histories.
assets.caption: Journey configuration and claim boundaries remain readable without horizontal clipping at the narrow viewport.

assets.altText: Desktop unsaved teacher journey with a visible cyan keyboard-focus treatment around required checkpoint visibility and honest empty current, causal, version, replay, and repair states.
assets.caption: Required visibility is keyboard reachable before the first immutable journey version is saved.

proofs.description: Focused source-inspection, role, checkpoint, and journey-authority gate examples
Five-hundred-run fixed-seed PROP-17 and PROP-25 UI action-gate invariants
Source-required confirmation, citation navigation, keyboard decisions, role denial, authority comprehension, ambiguity, rejection, history, and Axe coverage
Runnable teacher and student configuration, required visibility, causal lineage, replay and repair history states, keyboard focus, and Axe coverage
Existing fictional persona setup, likely-real-data refusal, reserved destinations, and per-run field isolation
Real browser, API, keyboard, focus, response, Axe, invariant, and source-bound visual assertions
Pure requirement-decision and journey-save gates consumed by the production UI and generated-state tests
Direct model proposal, human-confirmed rule, and observed browser fact explanation
Source navigation, focus return, role-aware decision readiness, human controls, and immutable requirement history
Role-aware journey gate, prerequisite chain, fictional persona, checkpoints, causal history, and separate replay and repair states
Existing Pactwire design-system treatments for authority lanes, source focus, decision state, responsive stacking, and replay and repair history
Curated red-green record, source-bound visual review, claim boundaries, and remaining completion gate

knownLimitations: Required clean-checkout Ubuntu and Windows GitHub Actions jobs have not yet passed for the UX-02 source commit, so the task remains IN_PROGRESS.
This task's journey editor truthfully displays empty replay and repair histories; presentation of populated run, finding, receipt, and hold records is owned by UX-03.
Automated Axe checks cover WCAG A and AA rules, while comprehensive manual screen-reader and WCAG 2.2 review remains QLT-01.
A confirmed requirement is a bounded human test instruction, not legal meaning, software approval, safety, or compliance; a runnable journey is not evidence that a browser run occurred or passed.
All browser evidence uses the controlled fictional district, fictional people, synthetic values, and reserved .invalid destinations.
